summary: Systems architecture review accepts the Keyword Expansion Agent slice. The implementation keeps prompts and agent behavior centralized under agents/, exposes typed Zod input/output schemas plus a stable public generation function, calls the existing safeJsonCompletion utility only through an injected LlmClient, and provides a deterministic local mock fallback with normalization, excluded-topic filtering, and count limiting.

review_focus.agents_boundary: PASS: prompt construction and keyword-generation logic live under agents/ and are re-exported through agents/index.ts; no UI/API component prompt scattering or scan orchestration coupling was introduced.
review_focus.public_contract: PASS: KeywordExpansionInputSchema, KeywordExpansionCandidateSchema, KeywordExpansionResponseSchema, inferred TypeScript types, and generateKeywordCandidates() provide a stable typed boundary for later scan orchestration.
review_focus.llm_boundary: PASS: the agent imports the existing services/llm public API and calls safeJsonCompletion only when an LlmClient is injected; no process.env, secret, fetch, provider configuration, or services/llm contract changes were found in this slice.
review_focus.mock_fallback: PASS: when no client is provided or safe JSON completion fails, deterministic template-based candidates are generated locally without network, database, paid service, or secret access.
review_focus.normalization_filtering_limits: PASS: candidates are normalized for keyword, country, language, rationale, intent, and tool fields; requested counts are capped; zero-count requests are preserved; excludedTopics are applied case-insensitively to keyword plus rationale text.
review_focus.persistence_deferral: PASS: deferring database writes is acceptable for this scoped task because the active task packet explicitly defers KeywordCandidate persistence to later scan orchestrator/API work.
review_focus.tests: PASS: focused coverage exercises LLM success, malformed/rejected LLM fallback, excluded-topic filtering, deterministic fallback output, and requested count limits.

decisions: Accepted the agents/ boundary as aligned with the architecture direction and suitable for later pipeline integration.
Accepted the typed Zod schemas and generateKeywordCandidates() public function as sufficient for the next scan orchestration slice.
Accepted injected LlmClient-only safeJsonCompletion usage because the agent does not configure providers, read environment variables, or touch services/llm internals.
Accepted deterministic mock fallback behavior as meeting the no-network, no-DB, no-paid-service policy for this task.
Accepted deferring persistence because the task notes explicitly narrow this slice to side-effect-free agent generation.
Accepted the test suite as sufficient for this architecture slice.
risks: The deterministic fallback is intentionally template-based; later SERP and scoring feedback may require tuning candidate templates for quality.
The case-insensitive excluded-topic guard uses substring matching, which is conservative and may over-filter edge cases; acceptable for the current safety-oriented slice.
Next.js build still emits the existing @next/swc-win32-x64-msvc invalid Win32 application warnings, though it exits successfully.
residual_risks: The deterministic fallback is intentionally template-based and may need product-quality tuning after scan orchestration exists.
The Windows SWC warning remains non-blocking but should be tracked if it starts affecting build reliability.

tests_run.result: Passed: 16 tests across 5 suites, including 5 Keyword Expansion Agent tests.
Passed: Next.js production build completed, type-checked, generated static pages, and exited successfully with existing non-fatal Windows SWC warnings.

requested_action: Proceed with QA acceptance review for M3-KEYWORD-EXPANSION-AGENT-001.
